{"step_by_step_thinking": "The query asks about enzymes elevated in the plasma in MI (Myocardial Infarction). From the documents, we can see that CPK-MB, LDH, and SGOT (AST) are mentioned as being elevated in MI. Specifically, Document [6] states 'Cardiac biomarkers are helpful in the diagnosis of AMI, particularly NSTEMI. Troponin, creatine kinase-MB (CK-MB), and LDH are cardiac markers observed.' Document [4] mentions 'LDH increases in the blood 6 to 12 hours after an AMI, peaks within 24 to 72 hours, and normalizes within 8 to 14 days.' Document [16] confirms 'If LDH-1 is found to be greater than LDH-2, it indicates myocardial infarction, with a ‘flipped’ ratio of LDH-1/LDH-2 greater than 1.' Document [24] and [26] discuss the use of CK-MB in diagnosing MI. SGOT (AST) is also mentioned in the context of MI in Document [4]. Therefore, all three enzymes (CPK-MB, LDH, and SGOT) are elevated in the plasma during MI.", "answer_choice": "D"}
